title: To direct the Attorney General to conduct a study on the efficacy of extreme risk protection orders on reducing gun violence, and for other purposes.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill tells the Attorney General to do a study about extreme risk protection orders. These are court orders that can temporarily take guns away from people who might be dangerous. The study needs to find out if these orders actually help reduce gun violence. The Bureau of Justice Assistance will do the research. The study must be finished within one year.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Study on Extreme Risk Protection Orders
sections.text: Within one year after this law is passed, the Attorney General must do a study. The Attorney General will work with the Director of the Bureau of Justice Assistance to complete this study. The study must look at how well extreme risk protection orders work to reduce gun violence. Extreme risk protection orders are court orders that can temporarily remove guns from people who might be dangerous to themselves or others.
